title: VideoProject
description: v2.0 — Unified, tool-agnostic JSON Schema for generative-AI video production up to 30 minutes. Merges the best of Grok, Manus, and Perplexity schemas and adds: cost tracking, retry/async logic, rights management, collaborative workflow (roles, comments, approval chains), QA gates, prompt versioning, platform delivery, localization, and accessibility.

version.description: Semantic version of the entire project; bump on any structural change.

scenes.description: Ordered list of scenes. Each scene contains shots; shots reference assets.

audio_assets.description: Map of audioAssetId → AudioAsset. Assets may be shared across shots.

outputs.description: Versioned final renders with provenance and delivery metadata.

dependencies.description: Explicit cross-asset dependency graph for orchestration and validation.

StatusEnum.description: Canonical status lifecycle shared by all versioned entities.

AssetRef.description: Pointer to any registered asset by UUID and type.

Rights.description: Rights, licensing, and IP provenance for any asset.

territory.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 territory codes where this license applies. Empty = worldwide.

GenerationCost.description: Cost incurred for a generation attempt.

RetryConfig.description: Retry behaviour for failed generation attempts.

fallback_tool.description: Tool to use if all retries fail.

fallback_asset_id.description: Asset to substitute if generation fails.

GenerationAttempt.description: Record of a single generation attempt (success or failure).

AsyncConfig.description: Webhook and polling configuration for async generation jobs.

webhook_secret.description: HMAC secret for verifying webhook payloads.

polling_interval_sec.description: Fallback poll interval if webhook is unavailable.

PromptRecord.description: A versioned snapshot of a prompt for A/B tracking and reproducibility.

Comment.description: Timecoded annotation or review comment on any entity.

thread_id.description: Groups replies under a parent comment.

timestamp_sec.description: If set, links comment to a specific timecode within the asset.

ApprovalRecord.description: A single approver's decision in an approval chain.

QaGate.description: Quality assurance checkpoint that must pass before assembly or delivery.

circular_deps.description: Descriptions of detected circular dependency cycles.

AssemblyErrorHandling.description: Fallback strategy when an assembly step encounters missing or invalid inputs.

PlatformDelivery.description: Platform-specific delivery metadata for a final output.

target_languages.description: BCP-47 codes of additional output languages.

AccessibilityConfig.description: Accessibility metadata and assets for WCAG compliance.

closed_captions_file.description: VTT or SRT file with closed captions.

audio_description_track.description: AudioAsset ID of the audio description track.

sign_language_track.description: VisualAsset ID of a sign-language overlay video.

RenderConstraints.description: Hardware and time constraints for the render pipeline.

max_cost_usd.description: Abort render if estimated cost exceeds this budget.

GenParams.description: Universal generative-AI parameter envelope — tool-agnostic. Typed fields cover the common surface area; 'extra' accepts any additional provider-specific keys.

prompt_version.description: Version tag for the current prompt, e.g. 'v1.2-variant-b'.

prompt_history.description: Ordered history of all prompt versions tried for this asset — enables A/B comparison.

attempts.description: Full history of generation attempts (including failures) for audit and cost tracking.

extra.description: Freeform bag for tool-specific parameters not covered above.

TemporalConsistency.description: Cross-shot and cross-scene consistency requirements. Research baseline for character_coherence_min_score is 7.99/10.

temporal_bridge_enabled.description: Conditions each scene's first frame on the last frame of the prior scene.

animation_method.description: How frames are produced when this shot or asset is animated. Only relevant when the shot is animated (video_style includes 'animation' or 'motion_graphics_explainer').

animation_playback_modality.description: Playback modality of the animated content (fixed, interactive, generative, or live-rendered).

scene_class.description: Python class name of the Manim scene to render.

QualityMetrics.description: Measured quality scores for a generated asset version.

asset_type_scope.description: Restricts permissions to specific asset types. Empty = all.

default_approval_chain.description: Default approver sequence applied to all new assets unless overridden.

alert_threshold_pct.description: Send alert when spent_usd / total_usd exceeds this ratio.

external_id.description: ID in an external system (Jira, Linear, Notion, etc.).

sunset_date.description: Date after which the project and its assets may be auto-archived or deleted.

video_style.description: One or more primary video styles for this project. Values are drawn from the formal taxonomy in claude-20-styles-video-v1.1.md.

characters_summary.description: Brief character roster; full definitions live in asset_registry.characters.

quality_rules.description: Hard constraints, e.g. 'no lens flare in Act 1'.

bezier_control_points.description: Four values [x1, y1, x2, y2] for CSS cubic-bezier easing.

layer.description: Z-order: higher values render on top.

anchor_frame_ref.description: Temporal bridge: last frame of the preceding shot used to condition this shot's generation.

depends_on.description: step_ids that must complete before this step.

python_snippet.description: Raw Python code for custom orchestration.

VideoStyleEnum.description: Formal video style classification derived from claude-20-styles-video-v1.1.md. Uses biconditional predicate definitions keyed on capture modality (κ), production intent (π), and distribution context (δ).

AnimationProductionMethodEnum.description: Animation production method classification derived from claude-animation-taxonomy-v1.0.md (field G in the animation artifact tuple A = (F, T, μ, s, ι, χ, β, δ)). Defines how frames are physically or algorithmically produced.

AnimationPlaybackModalityEnum.description: Playback modality classification derived from claude-animation-taxonomy-v1.0.md (field β in the animation artifact tuple A).
